segments.text: First listen, and then answer the following question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why do small errors make it impossible to predict the weather system with a high degree of accuracy?

translation.text: 为什么微小误差会使我们无法高度准确地预测天气系统？

segments.text: Beyond two or three days, the world's best weather forecasts are speculative, and beyond six or seven, they are worthless.

translation.text: 超过两三天，世界上最好的天气预报也只能是推测；超过六七天，便毫无价值。

segments.text: The Butterfly Effect is the reason.
For small pieces of weather, and to a global forecaster, small can mean thunderstorms and blizzards, any prediction deteriorates rapidly.

translation.text: 对小规模天气现象而言——而对全球预报员来说，雷暴和暴风雪也算‘小’——任何预测都会迅速失准。

segments.text: and uncertainties multiply, cascading upward through a chain of turbulent features, from dust devils and squalls up to continent-size eddies that only satellites can see.

translation.text: 不确定性不断增大，沿着一连串湍流现象层层向上扩展，从尘旋风和飑，一直到只有卫星才能看见的大陆尺度涡旋。

segments.text: The modern weather models work with a grid of points of the order of 60 miles apart.

translation.text: 现代天气模型使用的网格点大约相距六十英里。

segments.text: And even so, some starting data has to be guessed, since ground stations and satellites cannot see everywhere.

translation.text: 即使如此，有些初始数据仍须猜测，因为地面观测站和卫星无法看到每个地方。

segments.text: But suppose the Earth could be covered with sensors spaced one foot apart, rising at one-foot intervals all the way to the top of the atmosphere.

translation.text: 但假设地球表面每隔一英尺都布置传感器，并以一英尺的间隔一直向上延伸到大气层顶端。

segments.text: Suppose every sensor gives perfectly accurate readings of temperature, humidity, and any other quantity a meteorologist would want.

translation.text: 再假设每个传感器都能极其准确地读出温度、湿度及气象学家所需的任何其他数值。

segments.text: Precisely at noon, an infinitely powerful computer takes all the data and calculates what will happen at each point at 1201, then 1202, then 1203.

translation.text: 正午时分，一台能力无限强的计算机接收全部数据，计算每一点在12点01分、12点02分、12点03分会发生什么。

segments.text: The computer will still be unable to predict whether Princeton, New Jersey will have sun or rain on a day one month away.

translation.text: 即便如此，计算机仍无法预测一个月后的某天，新泽西州普林斯顿是晴是雨。

segments.text: At noon, the spaces between the sensors will hide fluctuations that the computer will not know about, tiny deviations from the average.

translation.text: 正午时，传感器之间的空隙会隐藏计算机不知道的波动，也就是偏离平均值的细微差异。

segments.text: By 1201, those fluctuations will already have created small errors one foot away.

translation.text: 到12点01分，这些波动已经在一英尺外造成微小误差。

segments.text: Soon the errors will have multiplied to the ten-foot scale, and so on up to the size of the globe.

translation.text: 很快，误差会扩大到十英尺的尺度，并以此类推，最终扩展到全球尺度。
